brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is in-place tokenizer/vocabulary expansion: continuing BPE merges on a multilingual corpus and initializing new embedding rows as the mean of source sub-token embeddings, followed by two-stage continued pre-training to cut token fragmentation. Paper B's machinery is a depth-separable neural network with cross-stage parameter sharing for nonlinear frequency alignment in mixed-frequency forecasting, backed by an approximation-theory error bound. Beyond the generic fact that both involve neural networks, there is no shared governing process and no coherent role-for-role mapping between BPE merges/embedding averaging and frequency-alignment sub-networks.
